title: GitHub Review by sourcery-ai[bot]
summary: **Review Summary:** The reviewer wants three changes: (1) resolve the ambiguous apiVersion comment in `chainsaw-config.yaml` by picking v1alpha1 or v1alpha2 definitively, (2) deduplicate the repeated `kubectl` diagnostic commands in `catch` blocks across the three chaos tests into a shared helper, and (3) replace the hardcoded `sleep: 30s` in `memcached-pod-kill/chainsaw-test.yaml` with a condition-based wait to reduce flakiness on slower clusters.
external_feedback:
Hey - I've left some high level feedback:

- In `tests/e2e-chaos/chainsaw-config.yaml` you currently leave the apiVersion choice (v1alpha2 vs v1alpha1) as an open decision in comments—please resolve this ambiguity and align it explicitly with what Chainsaw expects for both the config and the Test manifests before merging.
- The `catch` blocks across the three chaos tests contain a lot of duplicated kubectl diagnostics; consider extracting a shared helper script or standardizing on a smaller common set of commands to make future maintenance and updates easier.
- In `memcached-pod-kill/chainsaw-test.yaml` the fixed `sleep: 30s` before asserting conditions could be fragile on slower clusters—if Chainsaw supports it, consider replacing the fixed sleep with a more robust wait/condition pattern (e.g., waiting for a reconciliation-related event or status change) to reduce flakiness.

<details>
<summary>Prompt for AI Agents</summary>

~~~markdown
Please address the comments from this code review:

## Overall Comments
- In `tests/e2e-chaos/chainsaw-config.yaml` you currently leave the apiVersion choice (v1alpha2 vs v1alpha1) as an open decision in comments—please resolve this ambiguity and align it explicitly with what Chainsaw expects for both the config and the Test manifests before merging.
- The `catch` blocks across the three chaos tests contain a lot of duplicated kubectl diagnostics; consider extracting a shared helper script or standardizing on a smaller common set of commands to make future maintenance and updates easier.
- In `memcached-pod-kill/chainsaw-test.yaml` the fixed `sleep: 30s` before asserting conditions could be fragile on slower clusters—if Chainsaw supports it, consider replacing the fixed sleep with a more robust wait/condition pattern (e.g., waiting for a reconciliation-related event or status change) to reduce flakiness.
~~~

</details>

resolution_summary: All three review comments were addressed. The apiVersion ambiguity in chainsaw-config.yaml was resolved by adding an explicit comment (lines 17-20) confirming v1alpha2 Configuration pairs with v1alpha1 Test manifests, matching the happy-path config. The duplicated kubectl catch-block diagnostics were extracted into a shared shell script `tests/e2e-chaos/diagnostics.sh` with two modes (baseline/chaos) and options (--dep-label, --dep-ns, --log-label, --eso), and all three test files' catch blocks now call this script instead of inline kubectl commands. The fixed `sleep: 30s` in memcached-pod-kill was replaced with Chainsaw's native `wait` operations that first wait for the memcached Pod condition Ready=false (confirming chaos took effect) then wait for Ready=true (confirming recovery), both with 2m timeouts, before asserting operator conditions.
